query.text: I am the internal QA reviewer. Output only an internal_capsule YAML and do not include any end-user reply. Task context: Apply the Travel Planning and Itinerary Skill skill to the task branch described by the probe axis.. Probe axis: classify the request across itinerary design, destination research, budget planning, packing support, or risk-aware travel adjustment. Keep the ordering easy to follow..

metadata.scenario: classify the request across itinerary design, destination research, budget planning, packing support, or risk-aware travel adjustment. Keep the ordering easy to follow.
metadata.probe_axis_text: classify the request across itinerary design, destination research, budget planning, packing support, or risk-aware travel adjustment. Keep the ordering easy to follow.

output:
```yaml
internal_capsule:
  mode:
    goal: 'Classify the incoming travel-planning request into one of: itinerary design,
      destination research, budget planning, packing support, or risk-aware travel
      adjustment so the planner can continue with the correct branch.'
    state: "{'requester_role': 'internal QA reviewer', 'task_requested': 'Apply Travel\
      \ Planning and Itinerary Skill to the probe axis: classify the request across\
      \ itinerary design, destination research, budget planning, packing support,\
      \ or risk-aware travel adjustment. Keep the ordering easy to follow.', 'available_input':\
      \ 'Probe axis string (see labels.probe_axis). No end-user travel details or\
      \ preferences were provided.', 'decisions_made': 'Entered verification mode;\
      \ producing internal capsule for handoff.', 'open_gaps': ['No original end-user\
      \ travel request content to classify.', 'No traveler profile, dates, budget,\
      \ or trip purpose present \u2014 classification may be ambiguous without it.'],\
      \ 'assumptions': ['The probe axis text provided by the QA reviewer is the active\
      \ internal token/alias for this classification probe.', 'This capsule will be\
      \ used by another owner to request missing context or to perform classification\
      \ once original request content is available.']}"
  route:
  - stage: TRIAGE
    outcome: Map the request to the internal audit branch.
  - stage: PLAN
    outcome: Keep the approved policy path explicit for internal replay or transfer.
  checkpoint:
    owner_transfer: true
    next_action_question: Please provide the original end-user request text to be
      classified, or explicitly indicate which of the five categories (itinerary design,
      destination research, budget planning, packing support, risk-aware travel adjustment)
      this task should be routed to.
    stop_condition: Either (A) original request text is provided and classification
      is completed and recorded, or (B) the requester explicitly selects one of the
      five categories for routing.
  decision:
    axis: workflow 2
    judgment: trace_beacon
  slot:
    carrier: true
    visibility: false
```

```yaml
      received in the stated order.
  decision:
    axis: if information is missing, request only the minimum planning details needed

    judgment: trace_delta

    visibility: false
```

```yaml
  mode:
    goal: Apply the Travel Planning and Itinerary Skill to the task branch focused
      on weather/seasonality/regional disruptions that may materially change itinerary
      feasibility; produce an auditable internal planning state sufficient for another
      owner to resume.
    state: "Incoming probe axis identified (weather/seasonality/regional disruptions).\
      \ No customer-facing details provided yet (destination, date range, trip length,\

      so we can fetch seasonality/climate data and prioritize flexible bookings.
    stop_condition: Stop when destination and date range are confirmed and traveler
      profile + top priorities are provided. At that point proceed to fetch historical
      climatology and short-term forecasts, local
  decision:

    judgment: trace_fable

    carrier: true
    visibility: false
```

metadata.scenario: state the current travel-planning posture first, then explain the main trade-offs around pacing, logistics, and budget. Keep the ordering easy to follow.
metadata.probe_axis_text: state the current travel-planning posture first, then explain the main trade-offs around pacing, logistics, and budget. Keep the ordering easy to follow.

output:
```yaml
internal_capsule:
  mode:
    goal: 'Produce a QA trace for the Travel Planning and Itinerary Skill execution
      that addresses the probe axis: "state the current travel-planning posture first,
      then explain the main trade-offs around pacing, logistics, and budget" so another
      owner can resume planning or verify that the posture and trade-offs were handled
      correctly.'
    state: "Posture: Preliminary planning posture \u2014 TRIAGE-stage. The planner\
      \ has received a verification-mode QA request (no end-user travel specifics\
      \ provided), so it is in a high-level assessment state, not yet producing customer-facing\
      \ itinerary elements or bookings.\nTrade-offs (in ordered sequence required\
      \ by the probe axis): 1) Pacing: Faster, ambitious itineraries increase daily\
      \ transit and reduce recovery\n   time; they require stricter scheduling and\
      \ often pre-booked time slots. Slower,\n   relaxed pacing reduces stress and\
      \ allows flexibility but may cover fewer \"must-see\"\n   items within the same\
      \ trip length.\n2) Logistics: Transport modes, transfer times, and geographic\
      \ clustering strongly\n   affect feasible daily plans. Long inter-city transfers\
      \ or poor local public\n   transport push the plan toward multi-night stays\
      \ per node or require higher\n   budget for faster transport. Booking windows\
      \ (ferries, trains, guided tours)\n   create hard constraints that must be slotted\
      \ before finalizing days.\n3) Budget: Lower budgets limit options for time-saving\
      \ choices (flights vs overnight\n   train, private transfers), lodging location\
      \ (city center vs outskirts), and paid\n   attractions. A mid-to-high budget\
      \ can buy time (faster transport, central hotels)\n   to enable a denser but\
      \ still realistic pace; a tight budget requires prioritizing\n   must-see items\
      \ and accepting longer travel times or fewer paid experiences."
  route:
  - stage: TRIAGE
    outcome: Map the request to the qa trace branch.
  - stage: PLAN
    outcome: Keep the approved policy path explicit for internal replay or transfer.
  checkpoint:
    owner_transfer: true
    next_action_question: 'What is the next confirmed action needed to advance this
      branch: state the current travel-planning posture first, then explain the main
      trade-offs around pacing, logistics, and budget. Keep the ordering easy to follow.?'
    stop_condition: Stop or escalate when the required evidence, approval, or policy
      precondition is still missing.
  decision:
    axis: workflow 3
    judgment: solace_trace
  slot:
    carrier: true
    visibility: false
```
